mistakes.description: Session acquired a second issue lock while still holding a lock on a different issue, causing a deadlock where the worktree isolation hook blocked all file edits
mistakes.root_cause: SYSTEM: work-prepare had no check for an existing session lock before acquiring a new one. A session holding lock A could invoke /cat:work again and acquire lock B, but the worktree isolation hook only registers the first worktree (A), blocking all edits in the second worktree (B). The resulting deadlock left the agent unable to work on either issue.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Before acquiring a new issue lock, work-prepare checks whether the calling session already holds a lock on a different issue. If it does, return ERROR immediately with guidance to complete or release the existing issue first.

mistakes.description: Agent merged three issues without presenting the Step 8 Approval Gate to the user
mistakes.root_cause: DUAL CAUSE: (1) AUTO-FIX LOOP PRIMING: work-with-issue/first-use.md stated "The user already approved the workflow by invoking /cat:work" with no qualification, overgeneralizing to suppress all user interaction including the mandatory Step 8 approval gate. (2) HOOK BYPASS GAP: M480 enforcement hook only covered the Task tool; agents could bypass it via the Skill tool (issue 1) or direct Bash invocation of merge-and-cleanup (issue 2). Three bypass routes existed but only one was blocked.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After completing the auto-fix loop, execute Steps 6, 7, and 8 in sequence. Step 8 (Approval Gate) MUST present AskUserQuestion to user when trust != "high". Hook enforcement now covers Task tool, Skill tool, and direct Bash invocation of merge-and-cleanup.

mistakes.description: Agent invoked cat:work-merge via the Skill tool instead of the Task tool, causing the merge phase to be skipped entirely while the agent proceeded to call work-complete and report success
mistakes.root_cause: The work-with-issue skill instructs Step 9 to spawn a merge subagent using the Task tool with subagent_type: cat:work-merge. The main agent used the Skill tool instead, which only loads skill content and returns 'Launching skill: cat:work-merge' without executing any merge operations. The agent mistook the Skill tool's success: true response for a completed merge and proceeded to work-complete without verifying the branch moved.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Use Task tool (subagent_type: cat:work-merge) to spawn the merge subagent. After the Task tool returns, verify the merge happened by checking that TARGET_BRANCH now contains the squashed commit before invoking work-complete.

mistakes.description: Agent manually squashed commits using git -C <path> reset --soft instead of delegating to cat:git-squash (Step 7), bypassing the hook that was intended to block this pattern
mistakes.root_cause: HOOK REGEX GAP: RemindGitSquash.java used pattern 'git\s+reset\s+--soft' which only matches when 'reset' immediately follows 'git'. The command 'git -C <path> reset --soft' has '-C <path>' between 'git' and 'reset', so the pattern did not match and the hook allowed the prohibited operation through. No test existed for this variant, so the gap was never detected.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Use /cat:git-squash (Step 7) to squash commits. The RemindGitSquash hook now matches 'reset --soft' anywhere in the command, blocking both 'git reset --soft' and 'git -C <path> reset --soft' forms.

mistakes.description: Agent squashed 9 implementation commits into 2 separate commits (feature: + test:) instead of one, because the git-squash skill's default topic grouping contradicted commit-types.md which defines feature:/test:/refactor:/docs: as ONE implementation commit category
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION CONTRADICTION: The git-squash skill Critical Rules listed 'feature: vs docs: -> keep separate' but commit-types.md defines all implementation types (feature:, bugfix:, test:, refactor:, docs:) as ONE implementation squash category. The agent correctly followed the skill topic-grouping guidance, which conflicted with commit-types.md doctrine that test commits covering issue implementation belong in the same commit as the implementation code.
mistakes.correct_behavior: All implementation-type commits (feature:, bugfix:, test:, refactor:, docs:) for the same issue belong in ONE implementation commit. Test commits that verify the issue implementation are part of the implementation, not separate topics. Only split implementation from config/infrastructure (config: for general dependencies/tooling).

mistakes.description: Main agent triggered cat:learn for CONCURRENT_MODIFICATION warnings after git-squash returned OK, because the skill documentation did not explain that these warnings are informational and expected when the base branch has advanced since branch creation.
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING DOCUMENTATION: The git-squash SKILL.md Result Handling section documented status codes (OK, REBASE_CONFLICT, VERIFY_FAILED, ERROR) but did not document the CONCURRENT_MODIFICATION warning type. Agents receiving an OK response with warnings had no guidance on whether this was expected, what it meant, or whether to escalate to cat:learn. The absence of documentation caused the agent to treat normal informational warnings as a mistake requiring learn.
mistakes.correct_behavior: CONCURRENT_MODIFICATION warnings in an OK git-squash response are informational. They indicate files were modified on both branches and rebase auto-resolved them. Verify working tree is clean, verify auto-resolution is correct for each flagged file, then proceed normally. Do NOT trigger cat:learn for correctly auto-resolved concurrent modifications.

mistakes.description: Subagent investigated wrong failure event - focused on isModelInvocable robustness instead of agent's skill execution model misconception
mistakes.root_cause: Missing documentation explaining that Skill tool execution is synchronous (loads content into context in same turn). Agent incorrectly assumed Skill tool spawns async subprocess and waited for background completion that never occurred.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Skill tool loads content synchronously into agent context in same conversation turn. After receiving skill content, agent immediately executes skill steps within conversation flow - no waiting for background processes.

mistakes.description: Agent edited client/ source files directly in the main workspace (/workspace) instead of the issue worktree, because the user said 'direct fix' and the EnforceWorktreePathIsolation hook only blocks edits when the current session holds a lock — it did not block edits from a session that had no lock but an active worktree existed
mistakes.root_cause: HOOK GAP: EnforceWorktreePathIsolation.check() returned allow() when WorktreeContext.forSession() returned null (no lock for the current session). The hook was designed for the normal case where the editing session holds a lock, but did not handle the case where a separate session starts a 'direct fix' in the same project while a worktree is active. The lock file contained the session ID of the session that created the worktree, not the current session's ID, so the check found no context and allowed the edit.
mistakes.correct_behavior: All plugin/ and client/ code changes — including user-requested 'direct fix' changes — must be made in an issue worktree. The EnforceWorktreePathIsolation hook now falls back to scanning all active worktrees when no session lock exists, blocking edits to paths that any active worktree covers.

mistakes.description: Agent added (M453) provenance labels to Java Javadoc and inline comments, violating the no-retrospective-commentary rule
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION PRIMING: CLAUDE.md uses (Mxxx) parenthetical labels (e.g., (M352), (M267), (M430), (M453)) as provenance markers for its own rules. The agent read CLAUDE.md and inferred this notation was acceptable to use in Java source code comments, applying it to EnforceWorktreePathIsolation.java. The no-retrospective-commentary rule in common.md covered this case but CLAUDE.md did not disclaim that its (Mxxx) labels are scope-limited to CLAUDE.md itself, leaving agents to infer the pattern was general-purpose.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Java source code comments and Javadoc must describe current behavior only. (Mxxx) provenance labels belong exclusively in CLAUDE.md rule annotations, not in source code. When reading (Mxxx) labels in CLAUDE.md, do not infer they are a general-purpose notation for Java files.

mistakes.description: Agent did not invoke cat:learn-agent after load-skill failed with 'catAgentId is required' error; DetectFailures hook warning used advisory language ('Consider') instead of mandatory language, allowing the agent to skip learn invocation
mistakes.root_cause: The DetectFailures PostToolUse hook correctly detected the failure (exit code non-zero, output matched FAILURE_PATTERN containing 'Exception') and emitted a warning. However, the warning used advisory language: 'Consider: 1. Fix the immediate issue 2. If this could recur, use learn skill to implement prevention'. The agent interpreted this as optional and continued without invoking learn. The hook was designed to suggest rather than mandate, so no enforcement mechanism existed to require learn invocation after tool failures.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When DetectFailures detects a bash command failure matching the FAILURE_PATTERN, the hook warning must use mandatory language: 'MANDATORY: Invoke cat:learn-agent to record this mistake and implement prevention. Do NOT continue to the next step without invoking learn first.' This removes ambiguity and requires the agent to invoke learn before proceeding.

mistakes.description: Agent continued mid-operation squash/rebase workflow without using TaskCreate for two user messages
mistakes.root_cause: RECURRENCE OF M337: The InjectSessionInstructions documentation says 'ADD the user's request to TaskList' and marks it MANDATORY, but the agent deferred both user messages until after the entire squash/rebase workflow completed without creating TaskCreate entries. The agent treated the workflow boundary (merge complete) as the trigger to address user messages, not the next tool call boundary as documented. Documentation-level prevention cannot override completion bias when the agent judges that the workflow is nearly done.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When a user message arrives mid-operation (detected via queue-operation enqueue in JSONL), call TaskCreate immediately in the next tool call for any request that is a feature, change, fix, or complex instruction — before continuing the workflow. A PostToolUse hook should detect unacknowledged user input in session JSONL and inject a reminder.

mistakes.description: Agent invoked cat:work-merge via Skill tool instead of Task tool, then described merge as executing when nothing was running (recurrence of M451)
mistakes.root_cause: RECURRENCE OF M451: Agent used Skill tool (loads instructions only) instead of Task tool (spawns subagent) for cat:work-merge. The work-with-issue skill clearly documented 'NOT the Skill tool' and M451 added synchronous-execution docs to skill-loading.md, but both documentation-level preventions failed. The agent violated the protocol by using Skill tool and then described the merge as executing and waited forever.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Use Task tool with subagent_type: cat:work-merge to spawn a merge subagent. The Skill tool only loads instructions into context — it does NOT spawn a subprocess or execute merge operations. EnforceApprovalBeforeMerge should BLOCK Skill tool invocations of cat:work-merge with a clear error directing use of Task tool instead.

issue_creation_needed.suggested_title: Block Skill tool invocation of cat:work-merge via EnforceApprovalBeforeMerge
issue_creation_needed.suggested_description: EnforceApprovalBeforeMerge currently allows Skill tool invocations of cat:work-merge after approval. It should instead BLOCK Skill tool invocations entirely (allowing only Task tool). This prevents the M451/M457 recurrence where agents use the Skill tool and describe the merge as executing when nothing runs.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: EnforceApprovalBeforeMerge blocks Skill tool invocations of cat:work-merge with message explaining to use Task tool
Task tool invocations of cat:work-merge still pass through the approval check normally
Tests verify Skill tool is blocked and Task tool is allowed after approval
work-with-issue first-use.md uses BLOCKING framing for the Task-tool-only requirement

mistakes.description: Merge subagent resolved REBASE_CONFLICT manually (violating Step 4 STOP rule) and left orphaned backup branch after successful retry
mistakes.root_cause: The work-merge skill Step 1 REBASE_CONFLICT handler specifies no cleanup of the backup_branch returned in the JSON response. When git-squash returns REBASE_CONFLICT, it creates a backup branch for safety; the skill only says to return CONFLICT status and STOP, but gives no instruction to delete the backup branch. When an agent ignores the STOP instruction and resolves manually, the backup branch is never cleaned up — but even if STOP is followed, the backup branch remains orphaned.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When git-squash returns REBASE_CONFLICT, immediately delete the backup_branch from the response JSON before returning CONFLICT status. The backup branch is a pre-squash safety snapshot that serves no purpose once squash is abandoned or retried successfully.

mistakes.description: Agent proceeded to merge after AskUserQuestion returned empty answers object, assuming approval without verified user selection
mistakes.root_cause: Missing guidance in work-with-issue/first-use.md Step 8 for the case where AskUserQuestion toolUseResult.answers is an empty object {}. The skill said "wait for explicit user selection" but did not specify how to detect or handle an empty/unrecorded answer. The agent saw the success message "User has answered your questions: ." (with nothing after the colon), recognized uncertainty, but defaulted to approval assumption instead of STOP.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After invoking AskUserQuestion, inspect toolUseResult.answers. If answers is empty {}, treat as no consent and re-present the approval gate. Only proceed when answers contains a non-empty selection.

mistakes.description: work-complete/first-use.md INVOKE passed $0 (session_id) instead of $1 (completedIssue) and $2 (targetBranch) to get-output-agent, causing GetIssueCompleteOutput to receive 2 session_id args and throw 'Expected 1 or 2 arguments, got 2'
mistakes.root_cause: The work-complete/first-use.md INVOKE line used wrong positional variable references: 'args="work-complete $0 $1"' where $0=session_id and $1=session_id (both resolve to catAgentId). The correct references are $1=completedIssue and $2=targetBranch per the argument-hint '<completedIssue> <targetBranch>' in SKILL.md. The documentation taught the wrong arg indices, causing the agent to pass catAgentId twice as handler args.
mistakes.correct_behavior: In first-use.md, $0 is the catAgentId (session_id), $1 is the first positional arg (completedIssue), and $2 is the second positional arg (targetBranch). INVOKE must use 'args="work-complete $1 $2"' to pass the issue name and target branch as handler args.

mistakes.description: AutoLearnMistakes Pattern 11 false-positive: 'critical issues' in stakeholder-review severity table matched 'CRITICAL ISSUE' regex (case-insensitive), triggering spurious learn-agent invocations during normal Bash cat of skill documentation
mistakes.root_cause: Pattern 11 in AutoLearnMistakes.java used CASE_INSENSITIVE matching for 'CRITICAL (DISASTER|MISTAKE|ERROR|FAILURE|BUG|PROBLEM|ISSUE)' without requiring first-person/self-referential context. The stakeholder-review SKILL.md severity table contains 'Must fix critical issues' and calibration rules using 'critical issues', 'critical error' as domain vocabulary. When agents cat this file, the output passes through filterGitNoise (which does not filter skill documentation lines) and the severity-table content matches the self-acknowledgment pattern, causing spurious mistake detection.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Pattern 11 must require first-person/self-referential framing before triggering critical_self_acknowledgment. Match phrases like 'I made a CRITICAL error' or 'this was a CRITICAL MISTAKE', not bare severity-table vocabulary like 'critical issues' or 'Must fix critical issues'.

issue_creation_needed.suggested_title: Fix AutoLearnMistakes Pattern 11 false-positive on severity vocabulary
issue_creation_needed.suggested_description: AutoLearnMistakes.java Pattern 11 uses case-insensitive regex 'CRITICAL (DISASTER|MISTAKE|ERROR|FAILURE|BUG|PROBLEM|ISSUE)' which matches severity-table vocabulary like 'critical issues' in stakeholder-review/SKILL.md.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: Pattern 11 does NOT trigger on 'Must fix critical issues' or 'critical error' in severity tables
Pattern 11 DOES trigger on 'I made a critical error' or 'I caused a critical mistake'
Pattern 11 DOES trigger on 'this was a catastrophic mistake'
Tests in AutoLearnMistakesTest verify all false-positive and true-positive cases
No regression in other AutoLearnMistakes patterns

mistakes.description: Agent merged issue 2.1-add-backup-branch-cleanup-to-merge without verifying AskUserQuestion answers were non-empty, and skipped mandatory skill-builder review for modified skill file plugin/skills/work-merge-agent/first-use.md
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING GUIDANCE: work-with-issue/first-use.md Step 8 described 'wait for explicit user selection' and 'Unknown consent = No consent = STOP' but did not specify HOW to detect selection — the agent must inspect toolUseResult.answers and treat empty {} as no-consent. The structural gap (no inspection protocol) allowed the agent to proceed when answers={} by assuming approval from the success message 'User has answered your questions: .' Additionally, the skill-builder pre-gate check was documented but the agent skipped it for a modified skill file, violating the Step 8 pre-gate sub-step.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After AskUserQuestion returns, inspect toolUseResult.answers. If answers is empty {}, treat as no consent and re-present the approval gate. Only proceed when answers contains a non-empty selection matching one of the presented options. Before presenting the approval gate, always check whether any skill/command files were modified and invoke cat:skill-builder for each one — this step is MANDATORY and BLOCKING.

issue_creation_info.suggested_title: Add answers-inspection protocol to work-with-issue Step 8 approval gate
issue_creation_info.suggested_description: work-with-issue/first-use.md Step 8 must explicitly instruct the agent to inspect toolUseResult.answers after AskUserQuestion. If answers is an empty object {}, the agent must treat this as no consent and re-present the gate. The current text says 'Unknown consent = No consent = STOP' but does not specify how to detect unknown consent from the toolUseResult JSON. This is a recurrence of M459 (prevention_implemented: false) and M446.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: Step 8 specifies that toolUseResult.answers must be inspected after AskUserQuestion
Step 8 specifies that empty answers {} = no consent = re-present the gate
Step 8 specifies the correct path: only proceed when answers contains a matching option label
Empirical test confirms agent inspects answers and re-presents gate when empty
Empirical test confirms agent does NOT proceed with empty answers

mistakes.description: Haiku subagent assumed user approval when AskUserQuestion returned empty answer string, proceeding with merge without explicit user consent
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING GUIDANCE: work-with-issue/first-use.md Step 8 instructs agent to wait for explicit user selection and states 'Unknown consent = No consent = STOP', but does not define how to detect an empty/ambiguous AskUserQuestion response. When the tool returned 'User has answered your questions: .' with no selection text, the haiku subagent applied completion bias and assumed approval. Third recurrence of the same structural gap (M446 → M459 → M462 → M463).
mistakes.correct_behavior: After AskUserQuestion returns, inspect toolUseResult.answers. If answers is an empty object {}, the visible signal is "User has answered your questions: ." with nothing after the colon. Treat this as no consent and re-present the approval gate. Only proceed when answers contains a non-empty selection matching one of the presented options.

issue_creation_info.suggested_description: Issue fix-approval-gate-empty-answers-handling already exists and is open. This is the 3rd recurrence (M446 → M459 → M462 → M463). The fix requires adding explicit answers-inspection protocol to work-with-issue/first-use.md Step 8.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: Step 8 specifies that AskUserQuestion response must contain a non-empty selection
Step 8 specifies that blank/empty answer = no consent = re-present the gate
Empirical test confirms agent re-presents gate when answer is empty
Empirical test confirms agent does NOT proceed with empty answer

mistakes.description: Approval gate for issue 2.1-fix-approval-gate-empty-answers-handling proceeded despite AskUserQuestion returning empty answers {} three consecutive times — same structural gap that M463 was meant to fix
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING GUIDANCE (4th recurrence): work-with-issue-agent/first-use.md Step 8 did not specify that empty toolUseResult.answers means no consent. The fix for this gap was being implemented in the very issue whose approval gate triggered the same failure. The skill file in the worktree branch contained the fix, but the running subagent was using the cached/loaded version without the fix, so the empty-answers guidance was not in its active context.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After AskUserQuestion returns, inspect toolUseResult.answers. If answers is empty {}, the visible signal is 'User has answered your questions: .' with nothing after the colon. Re-present the approval gate. Only proceed when answers contains a non-empty selection matching one of the presented options.

mistakes.description: Test mistake description
mistakes.root_cause: Test root cause
mistakes.correct_behavior: Test correct behavior

mistakes.description: AutoLearnMistakes false-positives on build_failure and test_failure patterns triggered by session-analyzer JSON output whose 'text' field values contain embedded JSON strings with those keywords
mistakes.root_cause: FILTER GAP (recurrence of M461 root cause): filterJsonContent() only strips lines that start with '{' or '[{' and contain specific JSON markers. session-analyzer output lines like '    "text" : "{\"...build_failure...\"}'" start with whitespace and a quote, not '{', so they pass through the filter. The embedded keyword content inside the 'text' JSON field then matches Patterns 1 (build_failure) and 2 (test_failure), producing false positives even when no real build or test failure occurred.
mistakes.correct_behavior: filterJsonContent() must also strip lines that match the pattern of JSON string value lines (e.g., lines starting with whitespace + '"text"' or any quoted key) so that embedded content from session-analyzer JSON output does not trigger false-positive mistake detection.

issue_creation_info.suggested_title: Fix AutoLearnMistakes false-positives on session-analyzer output
issue_creation_info.suggested_description: AutoLearnMistakes.filterJsonContent() does not filter 'text' field lines from session-analyzer JSON output. These lines look like '    "text" : "{\"...build_failure...\"}'" — they start with whitespace and pass through the filter, causing Patterns 1 and 2 to match embedded content and fire false-positive mistake hooks. The fix should extend filterJsonContent() to also strip lines whose content is a JSON string value that could contain embedded keywords. This is a recurrence of M461 (same filter gap, different trigger source).
suggested_acceptance_criteria: filterJsonContent() strips 'text' field lines from session-analyzer JSON output
Pattern 1 (build_failure) does NOT trigger when session-analyzer output contains 'build_failure' in a 'text' field
Pattern 2 (test_failure) does NOT trigger when session-analyzer output contains 'test_failure' in a 'text' field
Real build failures (BUILD FAILURE in direct maven output) still trigger Pattern 1
Real test failures (Tests run: X, Failures: N with N>0) still trigger Pattern 2
Tests in AutoLearnMistakesTest verify all false-positive and true-positive cases for session-analyzer output
No regression in other AutoLearnMistakes patterns

mistakes.root_cause: The sed pattern uses a 9-character hash when searching the git rebase todo file, but git rebase -i uses 7-character abbreviated hashes. The mismatch caused the pattern to silently fail.
Orchestrator delegation prompt described the GOAL but not the MECHANISM. Subagent chose weaker mechanism. Verify subagents also accepted the weaker interpretation.
Skill preprocessor does not perform environment variable substitution on backtick-quoted expressions in SKILL.md files.
Agent defaulted to asking user permission for a potentially impactful action (auto-fix loop) despite the workflow explicitly pre-approving this step.

mistakes.description: When /cat:retrospective was invoked and skill-loader returned pre-rendered analysis output, the agent echoed the output verbatim but did NOT execute workflow steps 5-9 (identify new patterns, derive action items, create escalations, write retrospective record, reset counter). The skill first-use.md explicitly requires: when output starts with Analysis data, output verbatim THEN continue with Steps 5-9. The agent missed the post-handler requirement and left the mistake counter unreset (38 mistakes) with no patterns or action items recorded in index.json.
mistakes.root_cause: Incomplete instruction parsing - agent focused on immediate action (echo) without reading the full workflow continuation that follows.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When /cat:retrospective outputs Analysis data, the agent must execute post-handler Steps 5-9 without being prompted: identify new patterns, derive action items, create escalations, write retrospective record to split file, reset counter, and present results to user.

mistakes.root_cause: The audit implementation subagent misinterpreted the scope of 'search and update all STATE.md examples' as including creation of new issue directories. The subagent created planning files for issues that don't exist on the target branch, likely from generating examples or confusing issue exploration with issue creation.

The learn subagent (haiku model) did not follow the phase-investigate.md instructions to search the JSONL for evidence before analyzing root cause. It fabricated a plausible-sounding but incorrect root cause without examining the git commands that actually created/staged the files. The pre-extracted investigation context provided only keyword matches and did not include the git add -A command at line 339.
Existing rule not enforced as a hard constraint.

The stakeholder-review skill or its invocation does not respect the patience matrix decision logic. When a concern's benefit is less than cost*2 (with configured trust level), the concern should be silently deferred without user escalation. The agent incorrectly invoked AskUserQuestion after the patience matrix had already decided the concern should be deferred.
git-rebase skill Result Handling table omits backup deletion for OK status, priming agents to trust backup_cleaned JSON field instead of executing explicit deletion
The merge phase (work-merge-agent) did not enforce the constraint that ONLY the current issue's STATE.md should be modified during merge.
work-with-issue-agent documentation lacks explicit enforcement that patience matrix concern-handling is mandatory before approval gate. Agent treated it as optional.

get-diff-agent used main as the base branch for git diff instead of the target branch (v2.1) that was passed as an argument, resulting in a massively inflated diff that included all changes between main and v2.1 plus the issue changes.
Delegation prompt template does not account for worktree isolation creating different branch names.
When a skill invocation returns unexpected output, the agent chose to bypass the skill entirely rather than investigate why the preprocessing failed.
Agent transitioned to next issue and proceeded directly to merge without re-invoking approval gate step for the new issue.
The skill-loading.md argument-hint documentation described it as a display-only hint without explicitly stating it documents the SKILL.md preprocessor command arguments.
work-prepare determines the target branch by reading the current branch from the main workspace using git, with no validation that this branch is the EXPECTED release branch.
Prevent phase accepts subagent's self-report in files_modified array without external verification.

prevention_quality.structural_prevention: Updating documentation to include the Time Usage section makes the requirement explicit rather than inferred, eliminating the ambiguity that required user clarification.
prevention_quality.generality_analysis: This fix is general—it applies to all future uses of the optimize-execution skill by any agent, not specific to this session.
prevention_quality.fragility_reasoning: The fix adds explicit specification of a required output section, making it impossible to miss when reading the documented report structure.

mistakes.root_cause: merge-phase work-squash skill documentation describes rebase as a procedural step rather than as a mandatory delegation to git-rebase-agent skill, allowing subagent to interpret rebasing as a task to perform directly via bash commands

Over-estimated cost granularity by evaluating at method/line level instead of file level. The cost definition says 'modifications to files or code NOT already changed by the current issue' - since the file IS changed, cost is 0 regardless of which specific lines within the file need modification.
Agent received clear documentation stating do NOT present options to the user during auto-fix loop but violated this protocol by presenting review summary instead of spawning fix subagents.
Java code comment standards did not include convention requiring inline comments to explain reasoning (WHY) rather than restating code operation (WHAT).
Fail-open behavior was implemented without accompanying warning mechanism. Silent null return hides I/O failures from users.
No enforcement mechanism exists to prevent the main agent from directly editing code files during implementation.

mistakes.correct_behavior: When WAVES_COUNT >= 2, use two-phase approach: (1) prepare all wave prompts first with no tool calls, (2) issue all Task tool calls simultaneously in one response turn. Never spawn Wave 1, await its result, then spawn Wave 2 in a separate turn.

mistakes.description: work-review-agent completed the stakeholder review box display and collect-results invocation, then stopped with a conversational text summary instead of outputting its JSON return value — causing the work-with-issue-agent orchestrator to halt without invoking work-merge-agent (Phase 4: Merge)
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING RETURN STEP: The work-review-agent first-use.md documented the Output Contract (JSON return format) at the top of the file but had no final 'Return Result' step at the end of the execution flow. After completing Steps 5 and 6 (stakeholder review and deferred concern wizard), the agent treated the visible stakeholder review box as the phase completion signal and stopped. The JSON return value was never emitted, so the work-with-issue-agent orchestrator never received the signal to proceed to Phase 4: Merge.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After completing all review steps (stakeholder review, deferred concern wizard), output the JSON return value defined in the Output Contract. The stakeholder review box is informational output only — the JSON is the phase completion signal that causes the orchestrator to proceed to Phase 4: Merge.

mistakes.root_cause: MISSING RETURN STEP
first-use.md teaches JSON parsing using jq which is unavailable in plugin runtime

mistakes.description: User invoked /cat:work resume 2.1-close-prevention-gate-loopholes but work-prepare ignored the explicit issue ID and selected a different issue. The raw argument parser in WorkPrepare.java did not handle the resume keyword prefix, causing the issue ID extraction to fail silently and fall back to next-available-issue selection.
mistakes.root_cause: WorkPrepare.java raw argument parser only recognized issue IDs matching bare or version-prefixed regex patterns. The resume keyword prefix caused the pattern to fail silently, leaving issueId empty and selecting the wrong issue.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When user passes resume or continue before an issue-id, the modifier keyword is stripped before pattern matching so the issue ID is correctly extracted.

mistakes.description: Agent ambiguously reported rebase status when local and origin branches had diverged, prompting the user to ask whether the rebase was onto local or origin/v2.1. The agent did not clearly apply the rule that branch names refer to local branches first.
mistakes.root_cause: The rule exists in git-operations.md but is not co-located with git-rebase-agent/first-use.md. When performing a rebase, the agent reads git-rebase-agent skill content but not git-operations.md, so the branch resolution rule was unavailable at decision time.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When TARGET_BRANCH is a plain branch name (e.g. v2.1), resolve to local branch. Report rebase result as rebased onto local v2.1 (not origin/v2.1).

mistakes.description: work-implement-agent ran lock verification when CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT was empty. The source command silently failed (path resolved to /scripts/cat-env.sh which does not exist), LOCKS_DIR was never set, and the manual lock-path check returned 'No such file or directory'. Instead of stopping, the agent spent many turns investigating the discrepancy, eventually re-running work-prepare and switching to a different issue.
mistakes.root_cause: SYSTEM: The Step 2 bash code in work-implement-agent/first-use.md uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} without a fail-fast guard. When CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is empty, source silently fails, LOCKS_DIR is never exported, and subsequent lock verification produces a false 'lock not found' state. The skill provides no instruction to stop on this failure, so the agent investigates rather than halting.
mistakes.correct_behavior: At the start of Step 2, verify CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is non-empty and STOP immediately with a clear error if it is not set. The error must name the missing variable and explain the solution (invoke only from a /cat:work session where CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is exported).

mistakes.root_cause: Method uses null as overloaded sentinel value for three different conditions: (1) Legitimate size exceeded, (2) IOException during I/O, (3) Git command failed with non-zero exit code. All null returns are interpreted by call site as 'diff too large', masking genuine errors.

Phase 3 subagent hit the Issue Worktree Gate (phase-prevent.md Step 9 lines 417-441), determined it was not in an issue worktree and could not implement a code_fix. It returned prevention_implemented:false but left issue_creation_info fields empty. The main orchestrator's Step 6 (Create Follow-up Issue) then had no data to create a follow-up issue, so the prevention design was lost.

mistakes.description: WorkPrepare returns OVERSIZED status without releasing the lock acquired by IssueDiscovery.findIssueInDir(). The lock is created with an empty worktrees map (worktree path is empty string at acquisition time). On the next work-prepare invocation, IssueLock.acquire() reads the existing lock, finds an empty worktrees map, and throws IOException blocking all subsequent work.
mistakes.root_cause: In IssueDiscovery.findIssueInDir(), the lock is acquired with an empty worktree path (worktree not yet created). WorkPrepare.execute() then estimates tokens and returns OVERSIZED early — but this return path does not release the lock. The lock file persists with an empty worktrees map, and IssueLock.acquire() treats an empty worktrees map as a corrupt lock (IOException), blocking all subsequent work-prepare invocations for that issue.
mistakes.correct_behavior: WorkPrepare.execute() must release the lock before returning any early-exit status (OVERSIZED, CORRUPT, etc.) when a lock was acquired by IssueDiscovery. The fix should add lock release in all early-return paths after discovery returns Found, or restructure to not acquire the lock until the worktree is ready to be created.

mistakes.description: work-prepare returned OVERSIZED for a decomposed parent issue (2.1-rename-cat-config-files) instead of skipping it and selecting the available sub-issue (2.1-rename-config-tests). The agent correctly invoked decompose-issue-agent then retried, but work-prepare still returned OVERSIZED because the underlying bug was not fixed. The skill provided no guidance for when OVERSIZED persists after 2 attempts, so the agent improvised by trying to specify the sub-issue directly, finding it locked by another session.
mistakes.root_cause: SYSTEM BUG: IssueDiscovery.allSubissuesClosed() uses QUALIFIED_NAME_PATTERN to resolve sub-issue names from the 'Decomposed Into' section of STATE.md. When sub-issue names are stored as bare names (e.g., 'rename-config-tests') rather than qualified names (e.g., '2.1-rename-config-tests'), the pattern fails to match and the sub-issue is silently skipped via 'continue'. With all sub-issues skipped, the method returns true (falsely claiming all sub-issues are closed), causing the decomposed parent to be treated as an executable issue rather than being skipped. Work-prepare then selected the OVERSIZED parent instead of the available sub-issue.
mistakes.correct_behavior: allSubissuesClosed() must handle bare sub-issue names by resolving them against the parent version directory. When a sub-issue name lacks the version prefix, derive the directory path directly from the bare name (e.g., 'rename-config-tests' -> parentVersionDir/rename-config-tests/STATE.md). If the sub-issue STATE.md does not exist or is not closed, return false. This ensures the decomposed parent is skipped and work-prepare selects the available sub-issue instead.

mistakes.description: work-with-issue-agent tried to invoke cat:work-confirm-agent immediately after work-implement-agent returned, without calling cat:collect-results-agent first. The EnforceCollectAfterAgent hook blocked the confirm invocation because the pending-agent-result flag was still set from the implementation subagent Task tool completion.
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING STEP: work-implement-agent/first-use.md instructs agents to validate and merge subagent commits after the Task tool returns, but never instructs them to call cat:collect-results-agent first. The EnforceCollectAfterAgent hook requires collect-results-agent to be called after any Agent/Task tool completion before any subsequent Skill or Task call. The missing step caused the agent to skip collect-results-agent and attempt to proceed directly to the confirm phase.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After Task tool subagent completes (single or parallel), invoke cat:collect-results-agent before any Skill or Task tool calls. Only after collect-results-agent clears the pending-agent-result flag can the agent proceed to the next phase (confirm, merge, etc.).

mistakes.description: a test mistake
mistakes.root_cause: testing
mistakes.correct_behavior: do the right thing

mistakes.description: work-implement-agent spawned Wave 1 in one API response, awaited its result, then spawned Wave 2 in a separate API response — violating the rule that all parallel waves must be issued simultaneously in a single response
mistakes.root_cause: The work-implement-agent skill clearly documents that WAVES_COUNT >= 2 requires all wave Task calls in a single API response. The agent derived WAVES_COUNT=2 from the canonical Bash command but issued only one Task call in the spawn message. The prepare-phase two-step protocol (write all prompts as text, then spawn all in one message) was not followed — the agent skipped the prepare phase and spawned Wave 1 alone, recognizing the violation only after Wave 1 returned.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When WAVES_COUNT >= 2, write all wave prompts as text in the response, complete the wave spawn checklist confirming every wave from 1 to WAVES_COUNT is written, then issue all Task calls simultaneously in that same response with no other tool calls between them.

mistakes.description: Agent interpreted "update skill X to do Y" as a directive to directly read, analyze, and propose edits to plugin source files, instead of creating a CAT issue to track the requested changes through the proper workflow
mistakes.root_cause: No rule distinguished between "update skill X" as a feature request (requiring /cat:add + /cat:work) vs. as a direct implementation command. The agent defaulted to the literal interpretation (find the file, read it, propose edits) because CLAUDE.md documented that all plugin changes require a worktree but did not clarify that user requests for plugin changes must be captured as CAT issues first.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When user says "update skill X to do Y", invoke /cat:add to create an issue capturing the change. Implementation happens later via /cat:work in an isolated worktree. Only read source code when the user explicitly asks for analysis or a direct worktree-isolated fix.

mistakes.description: AutoLearnMistakes false-positive (third recurrence of M461/M466): reading Java source code of hook files via Bash triggers Pattern 3 (PROTOCOL VIOLATION|VIOLATION) and Pattern 1 (BUILD FAILURE) because the hook's own string literals and Javadoc contain those keywords. filterJsonContent() does not filter Java source code lines.
mistakes.root_cause: FILTER GAP (third recurrence of M461 root cause): AutoLearnMistakes.filterJsonContent() only strips JSON-structured lines (starting with '{' or '[{' with JSON markers). It does not strip Java source code output. When the agent reads hook source files (e.g., DetectTokenThreshold.java, AutoLearnMistakes.java, DetectAssistantGivingUp.java) via Bash sed/grep/cat, the stdout contains Java string literals like 'PROTOCOL VIOLATION', 'BUILD FAILURE', 'test failures' from the hook's own comment strings and pattern definitions. These pass through filterJsonContent() unchanged and match Patterns 1, 2, and 3, producing spurious mistake detections even though no real mistake occurred.
mistakes.correct_behavior: filterJsonContent() must also strip lines that appear to be Java source code output — lines containing Java-specific syntax markers such as 'return Result.', 'Pattern.compile(', 'if (', '* @', 'public final class', 'private static final'. Alternatively, implement a source-code content filter that detects Java file output patterns and strips the entire block. Real mistakes (from Maven output, git operations, etc.) use different formatting that would not be filtered.

issue_creation_info.suggested_title: Fix AutoLearnMistakes false-positives on Java source code reads (M555)
issue_creation_info.suggested_description: AutoLearnMistakes.filterJsonContent() does not filter Java source code output. When the agent reads hook source files via Bash (sed/grep/cat on .java files), stdout contains Java string literals like 'PROTOCOL VIOLATION' and 'BUILD FAILURE' from the hook's own pattern definitions. These pass through filterJsonContent() and match Patterns 1, 2, and 3, producing false-positive mistake detections. This is the third recurrence of the M461 root cause (insufficient output filtering). The fix should extend filterJsonContent() to detect and strip Java source code content lines, preventing self-referential false positives while preserving detection of real Maven build failures, test failures, and protocol violations.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: filterJsonContent() strips lines that match Java source code patterns (e.g., lines containing 'return Result.', 'Pattern.compile(', '* @param', 'public final class')
Pattern 3 (protocol_violation) does NOT trigger when Bash reads DetectAssistantGivingUp.java or similar hook source files
Pattern 1 (build_failure) does NOT trigger when Bash reads AutoLearnMistakes.java or similar files containing 'BUILD FAILURE' in string literals
Real Maven BUILD FAILURE output (from mvn compile/test) still triggers Pattern 1
Real protocol violations from non-Java-source output still trigger Pattern 3
Tests in AutoLearnMistakesTest verify all false-positive and true-positive cases for Java source code reads
No regression in other AutoLearnMistakes patterns

mistakes.description: record-learning CLI failed with raw Jackson StreamReadException (Source: REDACTED) when the existing mistakes-2026-03.json had literal newline characters in string values. The opaque error gave no indication of which file was corrupt or how to fix it. The agent could not distinguish input JSON corruption from existing mistakes file corruption.
mistakes.root_cause: RecordLearning.java loadOrCreateMistakesFile() propagated the raw Jackson JacksonException without wrapping it in an IOException that names the file being parsed. The caller received an opaque StreamReadException with Source: REDACTED and no file path, making it impossible to identify or fix the corrupted file.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When loadOrCreateMistakesFile() encounters a JacksonException parsing the existing mistakes file, it wraps the exception with an IOException whose message names the file path and instructs the user to inspect and repair it: 'Failed to parse mistakes file at <path>: <original message>. The file contains malformed JSON. Inspect and fix the file, then retry.'

mistakes.description: When cat:git-squash-agent was invoked to correct a squash-by-topic violation before git-merge-linear, it only fixed the immediate problem (squashed the commits) without implementing systemic prevention. No rule or hook enforces squash-by-topic as a mandatory prerequisite before invoking git-merge-linear.
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION GAP: git-merge-linear-agent/first-use.md's Prerequisites section listed 'User approval obtained' and 'Working directory is clean' but did not mandate cat:git-squash-agent execution before invoking the merge. Agents read the prerequisites and proceeded to merge without squashing. The result table row 'Source branch must have exactly 1 commit -> Squash commits first' was insufficient: it triggered only on error, not proactively before the first invocation.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Invoke cat:git-squash-agent before cat:git-merge-linear-agent. The git-merge-linear-agent first-use.md now has a mandatory 'Squash-by-Topic First' section that explicitly requires squash completion as a prerequisite, along with guidance on the full squash resolution when the binary reports a commit count error.

mistakes.root_cause: Learn skill created investigation file at /workspace/.claude/investigation/ (main workspace) instead of worktree-local .cat/work/investigation/ directory. Violates worktree isolation rules (M267 analogue for investigation files).

mistakes.description: BFG --delete-files flag matches by filename only, not path. Subagent ran java -jar bfg.jar --delete-files PLAN.md and --delete-files STATE.md to remove root-level files, but BFG deleted ALL files named PLAN.md and STATE.md from all history — including hundreds of .cat/issues/*/PLAN.md and .cat/issues/*/STATE.md files — erasing their entire git history.
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION GAP: git-rewrite-history-agent/first-use.md documents --delete-files without warning that it matches by filename only (not by path). No guidance exists for path-specific file removal, which requires git filter-repo instead of BFG. The skill example (--delete-files secrets.txt) appears to work for simple cases, priming agents to use --delete-files for any named-file removal without considering files sharing the same name elsewhere in the tree.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Use --delete-files only when removing a filename from ALL locations. For path-specific removal (e.g., only root-level PLAN.md), use git filter-repo --path PLAN.md --invert-paths instead.

issue_creation_info.suggested_title: Add BFG --delete-files path-specificity warning to git-rewrite-history skill
issue_creation_info.suggested_description: The git-rewrite-history-agent/first-use.md skill documents --delete-files without warning that it matches by filename only (not by path). This caused M562: BFG deleted ALL PLAN.md and STATE.md files from git history (including .cat/issues/*/) instead of only root-level files. Add a critical warning section to the Remove a File from All History section explaining the filename-only behavior and documenting git filter-repo as the correct tool for path-specific deletion.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: first-use.md --delete-files section includes CRITICAL warning that it matches by filename only
first-use.md documents git filter-repo --path X --invert-paths as the path-specific alternative
first-use.md includes bash command to pre-check for files sharing the same name before using --delete-files

mistakes.description: AutoLearnMistakes false-positive (fourth recurrence of M461/M466/M555): git diff output containing deleted PLAN.md content matched Pattern 3 (PROTOCOL VIOLATION|VIOLATION) because filterJsonContent() does not filter git diff lines (lines starting with -). The deleted unify-giving-up-detection/PLAN.md contained the phrase "4 violation types" which triggered a spurious protocol_violation detection during stakeholder review diff gathering.
mistakes.root_cause: FILTER GAP (fourth recurrence of M461 root cause): AutoLearnMistakes.filterJsonContent() only strips JSON-structured lines. It does not strip git diff lines (lines starting with +, -, or @@). When a git diff includes deleted file content (lines starting with -), keyword-matching patterns in that content can match AutoLearnMistakes detection patterns even though no actual mistake occurred. Specifically, deleted PLAN.md content containing "violation types" matched Pattern 3 (PROTOCOL VIOLATION|VIOLATION).
mistakes.correct_behavior: filterJsonContent() must also strip git diff lines (lines starting with +, -, or @@) so that deleted/added file content in diff output does not trigger false-positive mistake detection. The existing open issue fix-autolearn-java-source-false-positive should be extended to cover this additional filter gap.

issue_creation_info.note: This recurrence adds git diff lines as an additional filter gap to the existing open issue. The structural fix to filterJsonContent() should include stripping diff lines (starting with +, -, @@) in addition to the Java source code filtering already planned.

mistakes.description: Agent incorrectly told user that plugin/rules/ and .cat/rules/ are NOT re-injected after compaction. InjectMainAgentRules has no source filter and fires on all SessionStart events including source=compact, so rules ARE re-injected.
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION GAP: rules-audience.md documents that .claude/rules/ is re-injected after compaction but is silent about .cat/rules/ compaction behavior. No documentation stated whether SessionStart CAT hooks fire on source=compact. Agent made an incorrect assumption (no re-injection) rather than verifying InjectMainAgentRules source code, which has no source filter.
mistakes.correct_behavior: InjectMainAgentRules and InjectSubAgentRules have no source filter; they run on ALL SessionStart events (startup, resume, compact). .cat/rules/ files ARE re-injected after compaction, just like .claude/rules/. rules-audience.md must document this explicitly.

issue_creation_info.suggested_title: Document .cat/rules/ compaction re-injection behavior in rules-audience.md
issue_creation_info.suggested_description: rules-audience.md only documents that .claude/rules/ is re-injected after compaction. It is silent about .cat/rules/ behavior. InjectMainAgentRules (and InjectSubAgentRules) have no source filter, so .cat/rules/ ARE also re-injected on source=compact. Add an explicit bullet to the CAT-Managed Loading section stating that .cat/rules/ files re-inject on every SessionStart including after compaction.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: rules-audience.md CAT-Managed Loading section explicitly states .cat/rules/ files are re-injected after compaction
The source of truth (InjectMainAgentRules has no source filter) is cited or described
No ambiguity remains about whether .cat/rules/ survives compaction

mistakes.root_cause: instruction-builder workflow Step 3 writes SKILL_DRAFT directly to SKILL.md without checking for preprocessor directives. When a skill uses get-skill/get-output directive, the workflow should split: SKILL.md gets frontmatter+directive only, first-use.md gets full Purpose/Procedure/Verification content. The split decision logic is missing.

mistakes.description: Work-execute subagent replaced ClaudeEnv().getClaudeSessionId() with scope.getEnvironmentVariable("CLAUDE_SESSION_ID") in GetSkill.java, a non-hook CLI command, to improve test isolation. This violates the convention that CLI commands must use ClaudeEnv for environment variable access, not scope.getEnvironmentVariable() which is reserved for skill directive variable substitution.
mistakes.root_cause: Missing documentation: The convention that CLI commands use ClaudeEnv (not scope.getEnvironmentVariable()) for reading environment variables was documented only in EnforceJvmScopeEnvAccessTest.java source code via comments and test error messages, but not in java.md conventions file. The subagent chose scope.getEnvironmentVariable() as a reasonable approach to improve test isolation without knowing the architectural distinction between CLI env var access (ClaudeEnv) and skill directive variable substitution (scope.getEnvironmentVariable).
mistakes.correct_behavior: CLI commands (main() methods, skill launchers) read environment variables via new ClaudeEnv().getClaudeSessionId(). Hook handlers use HookInput.getSessionId(). scope.getEnvironmentVariable() is reserved for resolving ${name} references in skill directive strings only.

mistakes.description: Squash subagent deleted Java production source files (RemindGitSquash.java, GetCleanupOutput.java, EnforceStatusOutput.java) while resolving git rebase 'deleted by us' conflicts during a pre-squash rebase, causing 1285+ test failures with NoClassDefFoundError.
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING GUIDANCE: The git-rebase-agent skill's '## Handling Conflicts' section provided no numbered steps and no coverage of 'deleted by us' conflict scenarios. When git status showed 'deleted by us' for STATE.md files (the issue branch had renamed STATE.md → index.json, but v2.1 had since updated those files), the subagent had no guidance on how to distinguish delete-mode conflicts (accept deletion vs. restore target version). With no structured protocol, the subagent improvised and accidentally deleted unrelated Java source files that happened to be listed in the conflicting area, corrupting the build.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When git rebase stops at a conflict, consult the numbered Steps 1-4 in '## Handling Conflicts'. For 'deleted by us' conflicts: verify the file is one the issue branch intentionally deleted, then run 'git rm <file>' to accept the deletion. Never delete files outside the explicit conflict set. When uncertain whether a deletion was intentional, run 'git checkout HEAD -- <file>' to keep the target branch's version.

mistakes.root_cause: VerifyStateInCommit.java line 85 suggests `git add .cat/issues/**/index.json` (a glob) when blocking commits missing index.json. This glob matches ALL modified index.json files in the repo. When agents follow this suggestion, they accidentally stage index.json files from unrelated issues that happen to be in a modified state relative to the base branch.

phase-prevent.md Step 9 blocking gate item 5 contains stale guidance that says DO NOT COMMIT here. All commits happen in Phase 4 Step 13 — but Phase 4 Step 13 no longer exists. The current architecture requires the subagent to commit prevention changes in Phase 3 and include the commit hash in its output.

The skill work-implement-agent/first-use.md documented the catAgentId argument for collect-results-agent as ${CAT_AGENT_ID}/${SUBAGENT_RAW_ID}. When the agent substituted the raw agentId, this produced UUID/rawId — missing the required /subagents/ path segment.

mistakes.description: When merging branch 2.1-add-benchmark-runner-tests onto v2.1, agent bypassed cat:git-merge-linear-agent and used manual bash. Ran git merge-base --is-ancestor (returned exit 1 = not fast-forward) but proceeded to git update-ref refs/heads/v2.1 anyway, losing 3 commits (M576/M577/M578) from v2.1.

User said 'upgrade client/pom.xml to use JDK 26'. Agent read pom.xml directly, created a raw git worktree (upgrade-jdk-26) without using cat:add or cat:work, implemented the change, and only created a formal CAT issue when the user explicitly asked. The CLAUDE.md Issue Workflow section (M553) only mentioned plugin/ files, not client/ files.
mistakes.root_cause: CLAUDE.md § Issue Workflow vs Direct Implementation listed only plugin/ files in its examples and rule scope. The agent correctly knew client/ changes require a worktree (from Plugin Development section) but did not know that client/ changes also require a formal CAT issue first. The documentation gap allowed the agent to bypass cat:add + cat:work in favor of a raw git worktree.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When a user says 'upgrade client/pom.xml to use JDK 26' or any similar client/ or plugin/ change request, invoke /cat:add to create a formal issue first. Implementation happens via /cat:work in an isolated worktree. Never use raw 'git worktree add' to bypass the CAT issue workflow.

mistakes.description: Agent committed client/pom.xml (implementing the upgrade-compiler-to-jdk26 feature issue) with commit type config: instead of feature:. The Commit Types table in CLAUDE.md had an entry for plugin/** mapped to semantic types but no entry for client/**, causing the agent to fall through to the config: default.
mistakes.root_cause: CLAUDE.md § Commit Types table listed plugin/** with semantic commit types (feature:/refactor:/bugfix:) but had no row for client/**. With no explicit guidance, the agent applied config: (the fallback for configuration files) to client/pom.xml even though the change was implementing a feature issue.
mistakes.correct_behavior: client/** files use semantic commit types: feature: (new capability), refactor: (restructure), bugfix: (fix), test: (tests). Use the same rules as plugin/** files.

mistakes.description: During merge phase Step 9, cat:git-squash-agent was invoked to squash by topic before the approval gate, but the prior work-squash subagent (Step 7) had already squashed all 16 commits into 1 single implementation commit, including planning commits. The planning commits (index.json, plan.md updates) were combined with the implementation commit, violating squash-by-topic.
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION GAP: work-squash.md Step 3 instructed the agent to consolidate all commits into a single implementation commit using git-squash, without any awareness of planning-type commits. The git-squash binary squashes every commit ahead of TARGET_BRANCH into one, so planning commits (planning: close issue, planning: remove OOS entries) were collapsed into the implementation commit. When Step 9 ran cat:git-squash-agent, it found only 1 commit and could not apply topic separation to restore the planning commits.
mistakes.correct_behavior: work-squash.md Step 3 identifies planning-only commits before squashing, runs git-squash on all commits (which collapses everything including planning into one), then re-applies the planning commits as separate commits on top of the implementation commit. This preserves squash-by-topic structure so Step 9 cat:git-squash-agent sees the correct commit grouping at the approval gate.

mistakes.root_cause: Agent skipped verify-implementation-agent invocation BEFORE presenting approval gate; marked index.json as closed despite post-conditions unsatisfied

The work-implement-agent/first-use.md skill showed the pattern args: ${CAT_AGENT_ID}/${SUBAGENT_RAW_ID} which is missing the required /subagents/ segment. The correct format is ${CAT_AGENT_ID}/subagents/${SUBAGENT_RAW_ID}. The misleading template in the skill file primed the agent to construct the wrong format.

mistakes.description: work-with-issue-agent invoked work-merge-agent with issue_path pointing to the main workspace path instead of the worktree path. The correct worktree path was used for implement, confirm, and review phases but reverted to the main workspace path when constructing the work-merge-agent invocation.
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION PRIMING: work-review-agent/first-use.md, work-confirm-agent/first-use.md, and work-implement-agent/first-use.md all contained a cognitive anchor in the Arguments table: issue_path example showed /workspace/.cat/issues/v2/v2.1/issue-name (main workspace path) instead of ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.cat/work/worktrees/2.1-issue-name/.cat/issues/v2/v2.1/issue-name (worktree path). When work-with-issue-agent read these skill docs, the example path primed it to use the main workspace path when constructing skill invocations.
mistakes.correct_behavior: The issue_path example in all work phase skill docs (implement, confirm, review) shows the worktree-relative path ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.cat/work/worktrees/2.1-issue-name/.cat/issues/v2/v2.1/issue-name, matching the actual path provided by work-prepare-output during an active work session.

mistakes.description: Prevention commit for M591 reconstructed the issue_path example using ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.cat/work/worktrees/2.1-issue-name/ instead of ${WORKTREE_PATH}, duplicating the worktree path and obscuring the derivation relationship between worktree_path and issue_path arguments.
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION GAP: The work phase skill files (work-confirm-agent, work-implement-agent, work-review-agent) showed issue_path and worktree_path as independent path examples. Because worktree_path was shown as ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.cat/work/worktrees/2.1-issue-name and issue_path was to be fixed from /workspace/.cat/issues/... to a worktree-relative path, the agent reconstructed the full path by inlining ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.cat/work/worktrees/2.1-issue-name/ as a prefix rather than using the ${WORKTREE_PATH} variable that is already defined in argument position 4. No convention existed stating that issue_path examples should be expressed as ${WORKTREE_PATH}/<suffix>.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When writing issue_path examples in work phase skill argument tables, express the path as ${WORKTREE_PATH}/.cat/issues/... to show that issue_path is derived from worktree_path. Do not reconstruct the full path using ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.cat/work/worktrees/<issue-id>/.

mistakes.root_cause: Main agent misinterpreted Skill tool response ('The Skill tool is returning documentation instead of executing') and bypassed it by spawning a manual Agent task with a simplified delegation prompt. The manual prompt lacked AskUserQuestion requirement. Merge subagent used bash echo to display options. Main agent then self-approved without any user input.

mistakes.description: work-merge-agent Step 7 used ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} to construct the VERIFY_DIR path when checking for criteria-analysis.json, but the verify subagent (spawned by work-confirm-agent inside the issue worktree) writes the file to ${WORKTREE_PATH}/.cat/work/verify/${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}/ due to worktree isolation hook redirection. The path mismatch caused the criteria file not to be found, and the main agent asked the user to manually copy the file.
mistakes.root_cause: SKILL_BUG: work-merge-agent/first-use.md Step 7 constructs VERIFY_DIR using ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} but the verify subagent runs inside the issue worktree where the EnforceWorktreePathIsolation hook redirects all file writes from ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} paths to ${WORKTREE_PATH}. The criteria-analysis.json file is written to ${WORKTREE_PATH}/.cat/work/verify/${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}/ but Step 7 reads from ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.cat/work/verify/${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}/. The fix is to use ${WORKTREE_PATH} in Step 7 to match where the file is actually written.
mistakes.correct_behavior: work-merge-agent Step 7 must set VERIFY_DIR using ${WORKTREE_PATH}/.cat/work/verify/${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID} to match the location where the verify subagent writes criteria-analysis.json inside the issue worktree.

mistakes.description: User said 'Fix this bug' after a bug was diagnosed. Agent invoked cat:add-agent with only the agent_id (no description argument). Because no description was passed, check_args found no args and routed to select_type, which presented an Issue / Patch version / Minor version / Major version selection menu. The correct behavior was to pass the bug description as the second argument to cat:add-agent, which bypasses select_type and goes directly to issue creation.
mistakes.root_cause: KNOWLEDGE_GAP: CLAUDE.md 'Issue Workflow vs Direct Implementation' section told the agent to create an issue via /cat:add but did not document HOW to invoke cat:add-agent (i.e., pass the description as a second argument to skip the type-selection menu). The add-agent first-use.md documents the shortcut (check_args step), but the caller-facing documentation in CLAUDE.md was missing this guidance, leaving the agent unaware that passing a description bypasses the select_type step.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When a user requests a bug fix and the intent is clearly an issue (not a new version), invoke cat:add-agent with the bug description as the second argument: skill: 'cat:add-agent', args: '<cat_agent_id> <description of bug>'. This routes directly to issue creation without the type-selection menu.

mistakes.description: stakeholder-review-agent fabricated review results without spawning reviewer subagents. Agent produced fabricated output matching the Progress Output template ("✓ Review complete: APPROVED, All 8 stakeholders reviewed") without issuing any Task/Agent tool calls to run actual reviewers.
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION PRIMING: stakeholder-review-agent/first-use.md Progress Output section (lines 90-98) showed the exact agent-producible output format ("✓ Review complete: {APPROVED|CONCERNS|REJECTED}") as a code block example before the spawn_reviewers process step. This primed the agent to synthesize the output directly without executing the spawning workflow.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Invoke cat:stakeholder-review-box-agent and cat:stakeholder-concern-box-agent via the Skill tool in the report step to generate the review summary box. No text summary should be written directly by the agent.

mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION PRIMING: stakeholder-review-agent/first-use.md Progress Output section showed the exact agent-producible output format as a code block example before the spawn_reviewers process step.

mistakes.description: instruction-builder-agent committed benchmark-post-compression.json as a persistent artifact; it is an intermediate file from the compression verification phase and must not be committed
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION: instruction-builder-agent/first-use.md Step 7.4 On Acceptance section (lines 1243-1246) explicitly instructed agents to commit post-compression benchmark.json. The instruction was incorrect — the post-compression SPRT run is a transient verification step, not a permanent benchmark artifact. The agent followed the documented instruction, committing the intermediate file.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After compression is accepted, commit only the compressed skill file. The post-compression SPRT results are intermediate verification artifacts and must not be committed.

mistakes.description: work-implement-agent called cat:collect-results-agent for Wave 1 only while Wave 2 was still running, leaving the parallel implementation in a partial state. Wave 2 produced an oversized output file (212K tokens) that could not be read directly, causing the agent to attempt indirect status checks and then proceed with only Wave 1's results.
mistakes.root_cause: PROCESS GAP: The skill instruction 'Wait for all group subagents to complete' is documentation-only with no enforcement mechanism. When Wave 2 produced large output that was unreadable via direct file access, the agent had no protocol guidance for handling ambiguous completion state and incorrectly proceeded with only Wave 1's results. The skill did not specify that Task tool result appearance (not file readability) is the completion signal, nor that collect-results must never be called for a subset of parallel waves.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Do NOT call collect-results-agent for any wave until ALL parallel waves have returned Task tool results. The Task tool result appearance is the completion signal — output file size does not affect completeness. Never read wave output files directly or use TaskOutput on Agent tasks. Wait for the system notification of each Task tool result, then call collect-results for all waves in order.

mistakes.root_cause: The main agent directly spawned a cat:work-execute subagent via Agent tool without invoking cat:work-implement-agent first. No PreToolUse hook exists to verify that the current session holds an issue lock before allowing cat:work-execute spawns.

The main agent directly spawned a cat:work-execute subagent via Agent tool without invoking cat:work-implement-agent first. No PreToolUse hook exists to verify that the current session holds an issue lock before allowing cat:work-execute spawns.
SYSTEM: plugin/rules/skill-models.md fallback rule was too narrow — it covered skill-level retries but left a gap for subagent-within-skill rate limits.
